ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-11-2016 Continued To Jan-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For An Accounting; For Instructions; For Discovery
Off Calendar Nov-04-2015 Continued To Feb-22-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Kettelman for and with (petitioner) Anthony Braun. Daniel Byrne for and with (respondent/trustee) Mary Anne Ford. Kim Taylor (beneficiary) present in court.

The Court asked about the fair market value of the trust and if it qualifies for pro-bono mediation. Mr. Kettelman stated that it does qualify, that they would like to do discovery, that there is a failure of the trustee to fully account for the estate, that there are items in the home that are not community property, that the beneficiaries have been denied access, that there are missing items such as the grandfather's dresser, that there is a distribution of over $70,000.00 that respondents claim went to taxes, that there is a UBS account which is a non trust account, that the trustee took Mr. Savnik to sign off on Betty Braun's assets, that the trustee has an obligation to provide an accounting to the petitioner, that they have requested simple things that have been denied and that they want the tax returns for analysis. Mr. Byrne stated that they are willing to mediate but question the likelihood that it will be fruitful. The Court asked why not file for approval of an accounting and get the information to Mr. Braun so that the mediation will be fruitful. Mr. Byrne stated that an accounting has already been provided, that the tax payment is due to Betty Braun withdrawing money to buy her niece and nephew a home in Reno, that it was pre-tax money making is necessary to pay the taxes, that they are requesting the tax returns prior to Betty's death which the trustee does not have, that the trustee is only in possession of bank statements from September 2014 and on, that there is no allegation of wrongdoing by Ms. Ford, that Ms. Ford was not privy to the personal finances, that they make claims of undue influence on information and belief with nothing to back it up, that the time to contest the trust has lapsed, that they do not object to discovery and will cooperate in mediation, that this is the first time they are hearing about the issue with the grandfather's dresser and that the jewelry is part of the remainder and goes to the niece and nephew. The Court suggested setting a roadmap and the parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than February 17, 2016. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by February 17, 2016. The petition for an accounting; for instructions; for discovery was continued to and the Parties are to return on February 22, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The Court scheduled a status on filing a petition for approval of an accounting set for January 11, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Kettelman stated that Ms. Ford is also executor of the estate and has access to those returns and that if she will not supply them they will have to subpoena them. Mr. Byrne stated that they are dealing with the tax return for the period subsequent to the death of Ms. Braun, that the returns that the petitioner is looking for is prior to that and that they have no power to supply them. Mr. Kettleman stated that the discovery and depositions will depend on what is provided, that they may take the depositions of Mr. Savik and Ms. Taylor and that they will get the information expeditiously. Mr. Byrne stated that counsel is pursuing an attempt to get discovery other than what they can provide and asked if they are also pursuing undue influence. Mr. Kettleman stated that they do not know yet, that that is the purpose of the discovery and that they need certain documents to decide.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition For An Accounting; For Instructions; For Discovery
Off Calendar Oct-13-2015 Continued To Nov-04-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
